holdings.infoBox: PXJ is an equity ETF with a total of 33 individual holdings. The top holdings are Frontline stock at 5.66%, NOV Inc. at 5.12%, TechnipFMC at 5.04%, Baker Hughes Company at 4.91%, and Halliburton Company at 4.88%.

overview.description: The Invesco Oil & Gas Services ETF (PXJ) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in energy equity. The fund tracks an index of US companies in the oil and gas services sector that are weighted in tiers. The index uses a multi-factor methodology to select holdings. PXJ was launched on Oct 26, 2005 and is issued by Invesco.

data.title: Oil Supply to Rebound Strongly After Gulf Shock But Recovery Will Take Months, IEA Says
data.text: The Gulf oil supply shock is set to drag global oil demand sharply lower before flows through Hormuz gradually normalize, with supply expected to rebound to 8 million barrels a day in 2027.

data.title: Oil prices stabilise as investors weigh Iran war end, Hormuz reopening uncertainty
data.text: Oil prices edged higher in early trade on Wednesday, ​clawing back some of the previous session's losses, as investors assessed whether the Iran ‌war will truly end and the Strait of Hormuz reopen.

data.title: Oil Futures Rise on Likely Technical Recovery
data.text: Oil futures rose in early Asian trade on a likely technical recovery after futures fell for a fourth consecutive session on Tuesday.

data.title: US crude stocks fall for ninth straight week, sources say
data.text: U.S. crude ​oil inventories fell ‌for a ninth straight week last week, ​according to ​market sources citing American ⁠Petroleum Institute data ​released on Tuesday, ​while gasoline stocks rose.

data.title: Crude Oil Price Forecast: Breakdown Extends Toward Key Support Zone
data.text: Crude oil extends its decline after breaking key support levels, now approaching a major confluence zone near the 200-day and 200-week moving averages.

data.text: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley cut their oil-price forecasts with Persian Gulf flows expected to recover to prewar levels sooner than anticipated.

data.title: Oil Prices Fall Further as Trump Says Strait of Hormuz Will Reopen This Week
data.text: Brent crude and WTI prices were falling again as President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz could reopen as soon as Friday.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Cuts Oil-Price Forecasts on Faster Gulf Supply Recovery
data.text: The bank now said Persian Gulf exports could return to prewar levels as early as late July—about a month sooner than previously expected.
